title: Chronology and Record
subtitle: Official submissions, minutes, and related documents
summary: A dated record of the main Unicode and related SignWriting documents relevant to current compatibility and standards discussions.
synopsis: This chronology collects the main Unicode proposals, support letters, collation responses, design notes, and related records from 2011 forward. It is meant as a stable reference trail for historians, standards readers, and anyone who needs the documentary path rather than only the current position.

slides.title: Why the Record Matters
slides.summary: The Unicode discussion around SignWriting has a real document trail, and that trail matters for understanding the current technical and institutional position.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for chronology and record.
bullets: The record explains the present disagreement
slides.speech: The current disagreement is not built on one conversation or one proposal. It emerged across multiple drafts, replies, minutes, and later technical notes. If someone wants to understand why the current position is what it is, the document trail matters.
slides.notes: Keep this entry factual and documentary.
